cards.front: Three columns in `show ip interface brief` that matter for troubleshooting
cards.back: Interface, Status, Protocol (Layer 1 / Layer 2).

cards.front: Interface, Status, Protocol (Layer 1 / Layer 2).
cards.back: Three columns in `show ip interface brief` that matter for troubleshooting

cards.front: `administratively down / down` means?
cards.back: Interface shut by config. `no shutdown` needed. Both Layer 1 and Layer 2 are off because the admin disabled it.

cards.front: Interface shut by config. `no shutdown` needed. Both Layer 1 and Layer 2 are off because the admin disabled it.
cards.back: `administratively down / down` means?

cards.front: `down / down` (not admin) means?
cards.back: Layer 1 problem. Interface enabled but no signal — peer not configured, cable down, clock missing.

cards.front: Layer 1 problem. Interface enabled but no signal — peer not configured, cable down, clock missing.
cards.back: `down / down` (not admin) means?

cards.front: `up / down` means?
cards.back: Layer 1 OK (link detected) but Layer 2 protocol not negotiated. Encapsulation mismatch, keepalive mismatch, or one side still booting.

cards.front: Layer 1 OK (link detected) but Layer 2 protocol not negotiated. Encapsulation mismatch, keepalive mismatch, or one side still booting.
cards.back: `up / down` means?

cards.front: `up / up` means?
cards.back: Fully operational. Both Layer 1 and Layer 2 healthy. Pings can pass if IP layer is correct.

cards.front: Fully operational. Both Layer 1 and Layer 2 healthy. Pings can pass if IP layer is correct.
cards.back: `up / up` means?

cards.front: Default state of a serial interface on a fresh Cisco router
cards.back: Administratively down — must run `no shutdown` to enable.

cards.front: Administratively down — must run `no shutdown` to enable.
cards.back: Default state of a serial interface on a fresh Cisco router

cards.front: Default state of an Ethernet interface on most modern Cisco platforms
cards.back: Administratively down on routers, up on switches. Always check with `show ip int br` before assuming.

cards.front: Administratively down on routers, up on switches. Always check with `show ip int br` before assuming.
cards.back: Default state of an Ethernet interface on most modern Cisco platforms

cards.front: Default state of a Loopback interface after creation
cards.back: Up/up immediately — virtual interface, no Layer 1 to fail.

cards.front: Up/up immediately — virtual interface, no Layer 1 to fail.
cards.back: Default state of a Loopback interface after creation

cards.front: Two routers connected back-to-back over serial. R1 configured + `no shut`, R2 still default. What state shows on R1's serial?
cards.back: After `no shut`, R1 link flashes UP briefly, then drops to `down/down` (not admin). Reason: R2 hasn't enabled its end yet, so no peer keepalive returns.

cards.front: Same setup — now R2 gets IP + `no shut`. What happens on both ends?
cards.back: Both serials transition to `up/up` within seconds. Line protocol negotiates once both sides are alive.

cards.front: On point-to-point serial in lab, who provides the clock by default?
cards.back: The DCE end (Boson NetSim auto-assigns, real gear requires `clock rate <bps>` on DCE side).

cards.front: The DCE end (Boson NetSim auto-assigns, real gear requires `clock rate <bps>` on DCE side).
cards.back: On point-to-point serial in lab, who provides the clock by default?

cards.front: `%LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN` log message means?
cards.back: Layer 2 line protocol state change — most common Up/Down transition Romeo will see during lab bring-up.

cards.front: Layer 2 line protocol state change — most common Up/Down transition Romeo will see during lab bring-up.
cards.back: `%LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN` log message means?

cards.front: `%LINK-3-UPDOWN` log message means?
cards.back: Layer 1 physical link state change — fires before LINEPROTO when interface comes up.

cards.front: Layer 1 physical link state change — fires before LINEPROTO when interface comes up.
cards.back: `%LINK-3-UPDOWN` log message means?

cards.front: Two-way reachability rule for point-to-point links
cards.back: Both ends must be configured + enabled. One side alone = `down/down`. Pings fail until peer is alive.

cards.front: Both ends must be configured + enabled. One side alone = `down/down`. Pings fail until peer is alive.
cards.back: Two-way reachability rule for point-to-point links
